action.reasoning: Observation: cached CLIP features with a linear head gave strong local val worst-region accuracy (0.3748) but only 0.273 on hidden test, indicating overfitting to the OOD val split and likely insufficient robustness across regions/time. Analysis: the current linear probe is stable and cheap, so the best use of remaining budget is to improve the classifier on top of cached features rather than reintroduce fragile end-to-end finetuning. A high-value next step is region-aware model selection and ensembling on cached features: train a small MLP head with class-balanced loss, optionally blend with the linear probe, and select by worst-region val. This should be fast because feature extraction is already cached. Decision: rewrite train.py and predict.py fully (workspace copies are truncated) for a robust cached-feature pipeline supporting linear/MLP heads and simple ensemble logits, train an MLP probe on cached features for several epochs, evaluate locally on val, and if metrics improve submit the new checkpoint next iteration.
action.command: python train.py --data_dir /data --cache_dir feature_cache --epochs 18 --batch_size 1024 --lr 8e-4 --weight_decay 5e-5 --dropout 0.25 --hidden_dim 1024 --head_type mlp --label_smoothing 0.03 --weighted_sampler --region_alpha 0.7 --checkpoint checkpoints/clip_cached_mlp.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/clip_cached_mlp.pt --eval_split val --batch_size 512
